instruction: Create a Blueprint with an actor array Targets. On BeginPlay, loop through each actor, get the distance to it, and if closer than 500 print Nearby.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CheckNearby
PARENT: Actor

VAR Targets: Array<Actor>

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Targets]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetDistanceTo
NODE n5: LessThan [B=500.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Nearby"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.OtherActor [Actor]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CheckNearby
PARENT: Actor
VAR Targets: Array<Actor>
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Targets]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetDistanceTo
NODE n5: LessThan [B=500.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Nearby"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n6.Execute
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.OtherActor [Actor]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CheckNearby
PARENT: Actor
VAR Targets: Array<Actor>
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Targets]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetDistanceTo
NODE n5: LessThan [B=500.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Nearby"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n6.Execute
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.OtherActor [Actor]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint with an actor array Enemies. On BeginPlay loop through and find the closest enemy. Store the closest distance in a variable ClosestDist starting at 99999. Print the final closest distance.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_FindClosest
PARENT: Actor

VAR Enemies: Array<Actor>
VAR ClosestDist: Float = 99999.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Enemies]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetDistanceTo
NODE n5: GetVar [Variable=ClosestDist]
NODE n6: LessThan
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: SetVar [Variable=ClosestDist]
NODE n9: GetVar [Variable=ClosestDist]
NODE n10: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.True -> n8.Execute
EXEC n3.Completed -> n10.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.OtherActor [Actor]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n5.Value -> n6.B [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Condition [Bool]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n8.Value [Float]
DATA n9.Value -> n10.InString [String]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_FindClosest
PARENT: Actor
VAR Enemies: Array<Actor>
VAR ClosestDist: Float = 99999.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Enemies]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetDistanceTo
NODE n5: VariableGet [VarName=ClosestDist]
NODE n6: LessThan
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: VariableSet [VarName=ClosestDist]
NODE n9: VariableGet [VarName=ClosestDist]
NODE n10: PrintString
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n7.Execute
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.OtherActor [Actor]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n5.Value -> n6.B [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n7.True -> n8.Execute
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n8.Value [Float]
EXEC n3.Completed -> n10.Execute
DATA n9.Value -> n10.InString [String]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_FindClosest
PARENT: Actor
VAR Enemies: Array<Actor>
VAR ClosestDist: Float = 99999.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Enemies]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetDistanceTo
NODE n5: VariableGet [VarName=ClosestDist]
NODE n6: LessThan
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: VariableSet [VarName=ClosestDist]
NODE n9: VariableGet [VarName=ClosestDist]
NODE n10: PrintString
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n7.Execute
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.OtherActor [Actor]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n5.Value -> n6.B [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n7.True -> n8.Execute
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n8.Value [Float]
EXEC n3.Completed -> n10.Execute
DATA n9.Value -> n10.InString [String]

instruction: Create a Blueprint with vector variables PointA at (0,0,0) and PointB at (500,0,0) and boolean MovingToB defaulting true. On Tick, if MovingToB is true lerp toward PointB, else lerp toward PointA. When close enough to the target, flip MovingToB.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Patrol
PARENT: Actor

VAR PointA: Vector = (0,0,0)
VAR PointB: Vector = (500,0,0)
VAR MovingToB: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=MovingToB]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=PointB]
NODE n5: GetVar [Variable=PointA]
NODE n6: GetActorLocation
NODE n7: VectorLerp
NODE n8: SetActorLocation

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n8.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n8.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.A [Vector]
DATA n4.Value -> n7.B [Vector]
DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n7.Alpha [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.NewLocation [Vector]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Patrol
PARENT: Actor

VAR PointA: Vector = (0,0,0)
VAR PointB: Vector = (500,0,0)

VAR MovingToB: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=MovingToB]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=PointB]
NODE n5: GetVar [Variable=PointA]
NODE n6: Lerp [Alpha=0.02]
NODE n7: SetActorLocation

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n7.Execute

DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n6.Alpha [Float]
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]
DATA n4.Value -> n6.B [Vector]
DATA n5.Value -> n6.A [Vector]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.NewLocation [Vector]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Patrol
PARENT: Actor

VAR PointA: Vector = (0,0,0)
VAR PointB: Vector = (500,0,0)

VAR MovingToB: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=MovingToB]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=PointB]
NODE n5: GetVar [Variable=PointA]
NODE n6: Lerp [Alpha=0.02]
NODE n7: SetActorLocation

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n7.Execute

DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n6.Alpha [Float]
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]
DATA n4.Value -> n6.B [Vector]
DATA n5.Value -> n6.A [Vector]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.NewLocation [Vector]

instruction: Create a Blueprint that moves an actor back and forth along the X axis between 0 and 1000 units. Use a float variable Progress starting at 0. On Tick add DeltaSeconds to Progress, use a sine wave to calculate X position, and set the actor location.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SinePatrol
PARENT: Actor

VAR Progress: Float = 0.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Progress]
NODE n3: AddFloat
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Progress]
NODE n5: Sin
NODE n6: MultiplyFloat [B=500.0]
NODE n7: AddFloat [B=500.0]
NODE n8: MakeVector [Y=0, Z=0]
NODE n9: SetActorLocation

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n9.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.A [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.X [Float]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.NewLocation [Vector]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SinePatrol
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n6: MultiplyFloat [B=500.0]
NODE n7: AddFloat [B=500.0]
NODE n8: MakeVector [Y=0, Z=0]
NODE n9: SetActorLocation

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n9.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.A [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.X [Float]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.NewLocation [Vector]

PARENT: Actor

VAR Progress: Float = 0.0

instruction: Create a Blueprint with a float GravityScale variable defaulting to 2.0. On BeginPlay enable physics simulation, then on Tick apply a downward force multiplied by GravityScale every frame.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CustomGravity
PARENT: Actor

VAR GravityScale: Float = 2.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetSimulatePhysics [bSimulate=true]
NODE n3: Event_Tick
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=GravityScale]
NODE n5: MultiplyFloat [B=-980.0]
NODE n6: MakeVector [X=0, Y=0]
NODE n7: AddForce

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n7.Execute

DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Z [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Force [Vector]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CustomGravity
PARENT: Actor
VAR GravityScale: Float = 2.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetSimulatePhysics [bSimulate=true]
NODE n3: Event_Tick
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=GravityScale]
NODE n5: MultiplyFloat [B=-980.0]
NODE n6: MakeVector [X=0, Y=0]
NODE n7: AddForce
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n7.Execute
DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Z [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Force [Vector]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CustomGravity
PARENT: Actor
VAR GravityScale: Float = 2.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetSimulatePhysics [bSimulate=true]
NODE n3: Event_Tick
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=GravityScale]
NODE n5: MultiplyFloat [B=-980.0]
NODE n6: MakeVector [X=0, Y=0]
NODE n7: AddForce
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n7.Execute
DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Z [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Force [Vector]

instruction: Create a Blueprint that on pressing PhysicsToggle input, toggles physics simulation on and off using a FlipFlop. When physics turns on print Physics Enabled, when off print Physics Disabled.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PhysicsFlip
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="PhysicsToggle"]
NODE n2: FlipFlop
NODE n3: SetSimulatePhysics [bSimulate=true]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Physics Enabled"]
NODE n5: SetSimulatePhysics [bSimulate=false]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Physics Disabled"]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PhysicsFlip
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Physics Enabled"]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint with a Gate. On pressing Open, open the gate and print Gate Opened. On pressing Close, close the gate and print Gate Closed. On Tick enter the gate, and when the exit fires print Gate Active.

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n3.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint with a Gate. On BeginPlay open the gate. On overlap enter the gate. The gate exit prints Passed Through. On pressing Lock input, close the gate and print Gate Locked.
expected_dsl:

PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Passed Through"]
NODE n5: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Lock"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Gate Locked"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Open
EXEC n3.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n2.Close
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n6.Execute

NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Passed Through"]

NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Gate Locked"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Open
EXEC n3.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n2.Close
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n6.Execute

EXEC n5.Pressed -> n2.Close
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n6.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint for a damage zone. On overlap begin, start a looping timer called DealDamage every 1 second. On overlap end, clear the timer. Create a custom event DealDamage that subtracts 10 from Health and prints the health.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DamageZone
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="DealDamage", Time=1.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Event_ActorEndOverlap
NODE n4: ClearTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="DealDamage"]
NODE n5: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="DealDamage"]
NODE n6: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n7: SubtractFloat [B=10.0]
NODE n8: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n9: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute

DATA n6.Value -> n7.A [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.Value [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n9.InString [String]

instruction: Create a Blueprint for a healing zone. On overlap start a looping timer HealTick every 2 seconds. On overlap end clear it. The HealTick custom event adds 5 to Health, clamps to MaxHealth of 100, and prints Healed to X.

NODE n9: ClampFloat [Min=0.0]

instruction: Create a Blueprint for a collectible pickup. On overlap, cast to Character. If cast succeeds, add 1 to a Score variable, print the score, play a sound, then destroy the actor.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Collectible
PARENT: Actor

VAR Score: Int = 0
